competitor_patterns: Long-form content dominates: Oakley Forum ranks #1 for 'road cycling glasses review' with 8,608 words; Woman&Home ranks with 41,232 words — top competitors consistently publish 3,000–9,000+ word deep-dives with structured H2 sections covering sub-questions exhaustively

Comparison tables are standard in top-ranking cycling eyewear content — the Oakley Forum guide uses spec-by-spec breakdowns; our 'Best Oakley Alternative' post already has one but most of our posts lack them

FAQ sections are universal among top-ranking content (Woman&Home, Oakley Forum both confirmed has_faq: true) — treating every buyer question as an H2/FAQ opportunity captures featured snippets and AI overviews

Top competitors target year-stamped queries ('2026') explicitly in H1 and title tags — GQ, Woman&Home, and Oakley Forum all use the year in their titles to signal freshness to Google and match exact search intent

Competitor snippets lead with a direct answer or value proposition in the first sentence — Oakley Forum's snippet opens with 'Keep reading as we break down what to look for' signalling structured, authoritative guidance rather than product pushing
our_gaps: Photochromic post is dangerously thin at 1,104 words vs competitors averaging 3,000–8,000 words for the same topic cluster — Google is unlikely to trust it for competitive queries despite having FAQ and correct tags

None of our posts explicitly target 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses' as a primary keyword despite this being Velluto StradaPro's #1 hardware differentiator and a keyword cluster where Alibaba's broken 404 page ranks #1 — a massive content vacuum we are not filling

Our meta titles are not optimised for misspelling traffic: 'veluto' (292 impressions, 0.3% CTR, position 5.4) gets nearly zero clicks — we have no page or redirect strategy capturing this branded misspelling

Our posts lack explicit word-count depth on 'best cycling glasses 2026' despite ranking at position 8.7 with 215 impressions — the post targeting this query needs more direct comparison structure, a product table, and sub-sections covering road/gravel/commute use cases to compete with Zenni at #1

Zero geo-localised or use-case-segmented content (e.g. 'best cycling glasses for long rides', 'cycling glasses for wide face') — competitors capture these mid-funnel queries with dedicated sections or pages while we rely on generic post titles
keyword_opportunities: interchangeable lens cycling glasses — Alibaba's #1 result is a 404 error, creating a direct content vacancy; target with a dedicated StradaPro lens-swap feature post (1,500+ impressions estimated monthly)
best cycling glasses for road cycling 2026 — not covered as a standalone post; distinct from general 'best cycling glasses' intent, skews toward performance riders who are Velluto's core buyer
lightweight cycling sunglasses — StradaPro's 25g frame is a verifiable spec advantage; no competitor is leading with weight as primary differentiator in current ranking content
anti-fog cycling glasses — specific functional query with purchase intent; our anti-fog system is a built-in feature but we have no post targeting this exact query cluster

cycling sunglasses 30-day trial / try before you buy cycling glasses — no competitor owns this angle; Velluto's risk-free trial is unique and directly addresses the #1 objection to buying eyewear online unseen
next_post_guidelines: Target 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses' as tomorrow's primary H1 — open with the problem (changing light conditions mid-ride), explain the VellutoPuro/VellutoVisione system in a step-by-step how-it-works section, and include a comparison table vs photochromic lenses; minimum 2,500 words

Include a year-stamped title for every new post ('2026') in both the H1 and meta title — GSC data shows 'best cycling glasses 2026' at 215 impressions and 'best cycling sunglasses 2026' at 215 impressions; Google is rewarding recency signals in this category

Every post must open with a 40–60 word direct answer paragraph before any H2 — this is the format Google pulls for AI Overviews and featured snippets; structure it as: [query restated as statement] + [Velluto's specific answer] + [key differentiator in one sentence]

Add a comparison table in every post targeting a 'best X' or 'vs' query — minimum 4 columns (Feature / Velluto StradaPro / Competitor A / Competitor B) covering weight, lens system, price, trial policy, UV rating; Oakley Forum ranks #1 partly on structured data signals

Expand the Photochromic post from 1,104 to minimum 2,800 words — add sections: 'Photochromic vs Interchangeable Lenses: Full Comparison Table', 'Best Photochromic Cycling Glasses 2026 Ranked', 'When Photochromic Fails (and What to Do)', and a 5-question FAQ block targeting voice search phrasing

Embed specific weight and spec numbers in the first 100 words of every StradaPro product-adjacent post — '25g', 'UV400', '30-day trial' should appear above the fold to reinforce E-E-A-T signals and give AI answer engines quotable facts

Use H2s that mirror exact search queries wherever possible — e.g. 'Are Oakley Cycling Sunglasses Worth the Price?' rather than generic section labels; Oakley Forum's H2 structure ('What are the Best Oakley Cycling Lenses?') directly mirrors search queries and drives featured snippet eligibility
seo_quick_wins: Add a sticky 'Last Updated: July 2026' date stamp visibly near the H1 on all existing posts — Google's freshness signal is critical in the 'best X 2026' category and several of our posts publish dates may be suppressed in snippets without explicit update markup

Implement FAQ schema (JSON-LD) on every post that already has an FAQ section — our posts confirm has_faq: true but schema markup is not confirmed; this directly enables rich results and AI Overview inclusion for zero additional content work

Add internal links from every post back to the StradaPro product page using exact-match anchor text variants ('interchangeable lens cycling glasses', 'lightweight road cycling sunglasses', 'Oakley alternative cycling glasses') — current posts use brand-name anchors but missing keyword-rich anchors that pass topical relevance signals
meta_title_fixes: QUERY: veluto → New title: Velluto Cycling Glasses – Official Store | velluto-shop.com | Why: Captures the misspelling intent with correct brand + store signal; 'Official Store' adds trust and differentiates from resellers, lifting CTR for branded navigational searches

QUERY: best cycling sunglasses 2026 → New title: Best Cycling Sunglasses 2026: Tested on Real Rides | Why: 'Tested on Real Rides' mirrors the 30-day trial USP and creates curiosity gap vs generic list titles; year stamp matches exact query for CTR lift

QUERY: oakley alternatives → New title: Top Oakley Alternatives for Cycling 2026 (Lighter & Cheaper) | Why: Parenthetical with two concrete benefits (lighter, cheaper) directly answers the comparison intent and stands out in a SERP of generic 'alternatives' titles; 58 chars

QUERY: oakley sunglasses alternative → New title: Best Oakley Sunglasses Alternative for Cyclists 2026 | Why: Matches near-exact query phrasing, adds 'Cyclists' to qualify intent and reduce bounce, year stamp signals freshness; 56 chars

QUERY: oakley alternative → New title: Oakley Alternative Cycling Glasses – 25g & UV400 | Why: Leads with two verifiable specs to signal credibility immediately; differentiates from lifestyle fashion alternatives dominating this SERP; 51 chars

QUERY: oakley radar alternative → New title: Oakley Radar Alternative: StradaPro vs Radar EV 2026 | Why: Position 3.9 means a CTR-optimised title can capture significant click volume; direct model-vs-model framing matches high-intent comparison query; 55 chars
content_quick_wins: QUERY: best cycling sunglasses 2026 (pos 8.7) → Topic: 'Best Cycling Sunglasses 2026: The Complete Ranked Guide' | Add: A 5-product comparison table with columns for weight/lens system/price/trial policy, dedicated H2 per use case (road, gravel, commute), a 'How We Tested' section citing real ride conditions, and FAQ schema — current #1 Zenni is a homepage with zero cycling-specific content, a focused article should displace it

QUERY: oakley alternatives (pos 6.9) → Topic: Expand existing 'Best Oakley Alternative' post | Add: A direct spec table comparing StradaPro vs Oakley Radar EV vs Oakley Jawbreaker on weight/interchangeable lenses/price/trial policy; add H2 'Why Riders Switch from Oakley' with 3 real use-case scenarios; target 4,000+ words to match Oakley Forum's depth

QUERY: best cycling glasses 2026 (pos 7.2) → Topic: 'Best Cycling Glasses 2026: Road, Gravel & Sportive Tested' | Add: Segment by rider type (racer/sportive/commuter), include a 'Best for Weight Weenies' subsection leading with StradaPro's 25g, add a price-tier breakdown table (under €50 / €50–€150 / over €150), and embed 3 rider testimonials to strengthen E-E-A-T

QUERY: oakley radar alternative (pos 3.9) → Topic: 'Oakley Radar EV Alternative 2026: StradaPro Full Comparison' | Add: Side-by-side spec table (frame weight, lens options, nose pad adjustability, price, trial policy), a 'Ride Feel' section with tactile descriptors, and a verdict paragraph that includes 'Oakley Radar alternative' in the final H2 — at position 3.9 this needs a CTR-optimised title and a stronger opening paragraph to break into top 2

QUERY: best cycling glasses (pos 8.8) → Topic: Create a dedicated evergreen 'Best Cycling Glasses' pillar page (no year stamp) linking to all year-specific posts | Add: A master comparison table covering 8+ glasses, filter-by-use-case UX via anchor links, and a 'What to Look For' buying guide section of 600+ words — evergreen pillar pages accumulate authority over time and reduce dependence on annual updates
geo_angles: Structure every product-feature post to include a 'Quick Answer' box in the first 100 words using the format 'The best [query] in 2026 is [answer] because [specific verifiable reason]' — AI answer engines (Perplexity, Google AI Overview, ChatGPT) preferentially pull from content with a clear, early, self-contained answer that includes brand name + specific specs (e.g. '25g', 'UV400', '30-day trial') as quotable facts

Publish a dedicated 'Velluto StradaPro: Full Specifications & Independent Review 2026' page with a structured data table (schema: Product + Review markup) covering every spec — AI engines like Perplexity heavily favour pages with clean tabular data and explicit specification lists when answering 'what are the best cycling glasses' style queries, and this page becomes a citable source across all answer engines

Create a 'Velluto vs [Competitor]' hub page listing all comparison posts with a one-line verdict for each — ChatGPT and Perplexity frequently synthesise brand comparison queries by pulling from pages that explicitly position a brand relative to known competitors; a hub page with structured comparison summaries increases the probability of Velluto appearing in AI-generated 'top alternatives to Oakley' answers
comparison_post_angles: COMPETITOR: Oakley Forum (ranks #1 for 'road cycling glasses review') — angle: 'Oakley vs Velluto StradaPro: Which Road Cycling Glasses Are Actually Worth It in 2026?' — key differentiator: Oakley Forum's review is from 2022 and is brand-captured content; Velluto can publish an independent, updated 2026 comparison — Velluto advantage to lead with: 25g frame weight vs Oakley Radar EV at 26g, plus interchangeable click-in lenses vs Oakley's tool-free but single-lens system, plus 30-day trial vs no trial policy

COMPETITOR: Zenni Optical (ranks #1 for 'best cycling glasses 2026') — angle: 'Zenni vs Dedicated Cycling Glasses: Why Generic Eyewear Fails on the Bike (2026)' — key differentiator: Zenni is a prescription/fashion brand with zero cycling-specific features; Velluto can own the 'purpose-built vs generic' positioning — Velluto advantage to lead with: anti-fog system, UV400 certification for outdoor sport, interchangeable sport lenses, and aerodynamic Italian design vs fashion frames not built for speed or sweat

COMPETITOR: Woman&Home / GQ Magazine (ranks for eyewear trend content) — angle: 'Style Meets Performance: The Best-Looking Cycling Glasses of 2026 (That Actually Work)' — key differentiator: fashion/lifestyle publications cover aesthetics but never performance specs; Velluto bridges Italian design and sport function — Velluto advantage to lead with: Italian design aesthetic + adjustable nose pads for custom fit + 25g ultralight frame, positioning StradaPro as the cycling glasses that looks good on and off the bike without sacrificing performance
